series high_income = income > median(income)
diff food_exp high_income
pi_items.verified_value:
open food.gdt
series high_income = income > median(income)

pi_items.source: Verified by manual implementation: script correctly creates high_income dummy and performs two-sample t-test on food_exp between groups

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is real, from Hill, Griffiths and Lim's Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (POE5), containing 40 observations of household survey data with two variables: food_exp (weekly food expenditure in dollars) and income (weekly income in $100 units). The median income is 13.29 ($100 units). When grouped by high_income status (income > median), the low-income group (20 observations) has mean food expenditure of $186.44, while the high-income group (20 observations) has mean of $321.76. A two-sample t-test comparing food expenditure between these groups yields t-statistic = -8.887250 with p-value < 0.000001 and 38 degrees of freedom, indicating highly significant difference in food spending between income groups. All metadata claims are verified against actual data and source code.
